heading: Signing of returns and other documents
label: 6061 Signing of returns and other documents

children.heading: General rule
children.label: a General rule
children.text: Except as otherwise provided by subsection (b) and sections 6062 and 6063, any return, statement, or other document required to be made under any provision of the internal revenue laws or regulations shall be signed in accordance with forms or regulations prescribed by the Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: waive the requirement of a signature for; or

children.label: B
children.text: provide for alternative methods of signing or subscribing,

children.heading: Treatment of alternative methods
children.label: 2 Treatment of alternative methods
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, any return, declaration, statement, or other document filed and verified, signed, or subscribed under any method adopted under paragraph (1)(B) shall be treated for all purposes (both civil and criminal, including penalties for perjury) in the same manner as though signed or subscribed.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall publish guidance as appropriate to define and implement any waiver of the signature requirements or any method adopted under paragraph (1).

children.heading: Electronic signatures for disclosure authorizations to, and other authorizations of, practitioners
children.label: B Electronic signatures for disclosure authorizations to, and other authorizations of, practitioners
children.text: Not later than 6 months after the date of the enactment of this subparagraph, the Secretary shall publish guidance to establish uniform standards and procedures for the acceptance of taxpayers’ signatures appearing in electronic form with respect to any request for disclosure of a taxpayer’s return or return information under section 6103(c) to a practitioner or any power of attorney granted by a taxpayer to a practitioner.

children.heading: Practitioner
children.label: C Practitioner
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (B), the term “practitioner” means any individual in good standing who is regulated under , United States Code.section 330 of title 31
